channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn will launch on Sunday—initiating Bezos’s challenge to SpaceX
item.description: Jeff Bezos‘s Blue Origin is set for an inaugural launch of its giant New Glenn rocket on Sunday, a long-awaited first leap to Earth orbit that sets up one of the biggest challenges yet to industry dominance enjoyed by Elon Musk‘s SpaceX.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: This video company is positioning itself for a world without TikTok
item.description: As TikTok pleads with the U.S. Supreme Court to let it continue operating in the United States, one video company is taking advantage of the uncertainty around TikTok’s sister app.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok’s future looks bleak
item.description: TikTok’s future prospects in the United States looked grimmer than ever Friday, following a bruising day of Supreme Court arguments in a case that could determine whether the app is forced to shut down on January 19.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Industrial routers are being hit by zero-days from new Mirai botnets
item.description: A new botnet, based on the dreaded Mirai, is actively assimilating devices, including smart home gadgets and industrial routers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Zuckerberg says Biden officials would 'scream,' 'curse' at Meta team over COVID takedown requests
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said Biden administration officials would “scream” and “curse” at his employees when they disagreed with the government’s takedown requests over pandemic-related content.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: As Prime Video wraps up its third season doing NFL games with an AFC wild-card matchup between the Pittsburgh Steelers and Baltimore Ravens on Saturday night, the “Prime Vision” feed and use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the broadcasts continue to change the way football fans are able to analyz
item.dc:creator: Joe Reedy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Red Bull and Ford are building a new F1 hybrid race car engine—first as bits, then atoms
item.description: To get from 0 to 60 in Formula 1 engine design while competing against organizations with much more experience, Red Bull Ford Powertrains will need extra help (and, no, that boost won’t come in the form of a certain energy drink).
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: Lenovo Establishes New MEA HQ in Saudi Arabia, to Manufacture Millions of PCs and Servers
item.description: Lenovo announces plans to establish its new Middle East and Africa (MEA) headquarters in Saudi Arabia, with the goal of manufacturing millions of PCs and servers. The company's financial performance is strong, with revenues rising 15% for its Intelligent Devices Group.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: H-1B: Silicon Valley’s favorite foreign-worker visa under attack, while Trump, Musk offer support
item.description: The schism in MAGA raised questions about the fate in the second Trump era of a visa seen in Silicon Valley as key to the technology industry's innovation and success. It comes at a time when the tech industry has gained unprecedented proximity to U.S. levers of power.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Linux Foundation brings together top browser makers for more "open" approach
item.description: Google, Meta, Microsoft and Opera have already joined the Supporters of Chromium-Based Browsers initiative.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AMD blames Ryzen 9800X3D stock shortage on Intel’s ‘horrible’ Arrow Lake launch, rubbing salt in Team Blue’s CPU wounds
item.description: Can’t find a Ryzen 9800X3D CPU for your gaming PC? That’s Intel’s fault, AMD implies, for making Arrow Lake so bad.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Enterprises willing to spend up to $250 million on gen AI, but ROI remains elusive
item.description: A sharp rise in enterprise investments in generative AI is poised to reshape business operations, with 68% of companies planning to invest between $50 million and $250 million over the next year, according to KPMG’s latest AI Quarterly Pulse Survey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to easily print photos from your phone
item.description: Most cameras allow you to instantly transfer images from a camera to a smartphone via Bluetooth, and you can print photos directly from your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dell’s new PC names are boring—and a smart move
item.description: There are surely many reasons that the parents of tech icon Michael Dell did not name him Inspiron or XPS. For one, Dell’s parents aren’t Elon Musk. More generally, there is appeal to a commonplace name. Yes, you sacrifice originality. You risk being called a copycat by your cousin.
item.dc:creator: Katy Steinmetz, Fast Company

item.title: Researchers build a bridge from C to Rust and memory safety
item.description: Memory errors such as out-of-bounds reads and writes and use-after-free bugs have plagued applications for decades, causing problems ranging from minor execution glitches to global security nightmares.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Flying taxis are on the horizon as aviation soars into a new frontier
item.description: Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation and nearby Wisk Aero, with ties to aerospace giant Boeing Co. and Google co-founder Larry Page, are at the forefront in the race to bring air taxis to market in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Smart Ring Review: A Comprehensive Look at RingConn Gen 2
item.description: A detailed review of RingConn Gen 2 smart ring, a competitor to Oura Ring, highlighting its features, accuracy, and pricing compared to other fitness trackers.
item.dc:creator: Larry Magid, The Mercury News

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok's fate arrives at Supreme Court in collision of free speech and national security
item.description: In a collision between free speech and national security, the Supreme Court will hear arguments over the fate of TikTok, a digital age cultural phenomenon that roughly half the U.S. population uses for entertainment and information.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nvidia launches blueprints to help jump start AI projects
item.description: Give Nvidia credit, it’s not just talking up big ideas, it’s helping its customers achieve them. The vendor recently issued designs for AI factories after hyping up the idea for several months.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Flying taxis are on the horizon as aviation soars into a new frontier
item.description: “We are just a few steps from the finish line. We want to turn what are now one- and two-hour trips into five-minute trips,” Bevirt, 51, said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Oracle offers price-performance boost with Exadata X11M update
item.description: The new version of Oracle’s Exadata platform, X11M, is now generally available, and promises improved performance on generative AI tasks such as retrieval augmented generation (RAG) for the same price as the old version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI detects ovarian cancer better than human doctors: Study
item.description: AI based models achieved an accuracy rate of 86% for detecting ovarian cancer, compared to 82% for human experts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: Editor’s note: This is an archive of 2024 incidents as tracked by Cisco subsidiary ThousandEyes. For current trends, see the 2025 outage report and internet health check, which is updated weekly.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: It is easier than ever to disable Location Sharing on your Android phone
item.description: Google Maps Location Sharing is now built into Android settings; no need for Maps settings. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses the welcome improvement for Android users.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation
